rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HIDEO IWATA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/6/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The fourth amended petition was not e-filed until 10/23/24, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. This has been a repeated issue. Notice may NOT BE MAILED until the pleading requiring notice is E-FILED and available for viewing. [Please review LRSF 14.14 A.] (2) It is unclear how the appraised value of [tangible] personal property was determined to be $12,370.00. (3) How was the loss on sale determined to be $6,065.00? (4) Detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sale must be filed to support the statutory fee. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] (5) The administrator sold some of the tangible personal property to her attorney. Under what authority? [Probate Code Section 10501(a)(5). (6) Did the administrator give notice of proposed action to abandon the "unsellable" tangible personal property by donating to charity? [Probate Code Section 10513.] (7) The list of items reportedly purchased by Cresalia Jewelers (notably described as "PG's listing of items," not the administrator's) would be better supported if the sale/purchase documents from Cresalia Jewelers were filed, not the administrator's handwritten notes. (8) The administrator has been maintaining cash in excess of the FDIC amount at one bank. Deadline is 12/23/24 to provide the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The copy of the fourth amended petition provided to the examiner was not a true and correct copy; most of the waivers of accounting were not attached.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GERARDO SORRENTINO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/9/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Please file a copy of Letters certified no more than 60 days before the continued hearing date and issued to the executor of Jeannette N. Dupont's will, Paul Joseph Hermelin. [LRSF 14.35 I.6.] (2) If the shares distributable to the wife's heirs are calculated on the full net proceeds of sale of the real property and the balance of just the Bank of America money market account on hand as of the date of death, will they not be liable for any portion of the statutory fee and costs? (3) Is the Bank of America checking account not included in the 50% community property interest because the decedent paid real property expenses after his wife's death? (It is not entirely clear why the two community property bank accounts were not both included.) (4) The $81 filing fee for Letters was paid out of the estate already, as shown on the disbursements schedule. Executor's reimbursable expenses (not set forth specifically in the report, only in the prayer, and attached as Exhibit B to the petition) must be reduced by $81.00. That amount could be either added to the $40,000 reserve or added back in to the assets proposed to be distributed. (E-filing fees should be itemized to show what was e-filed, not as a lump sum amount.)ar why random bank statements were inserted in the accounting. Only the original statements verifying the ending balance are required. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (5) If there is any other after-discovered community property asset, the wife's heirs would be entitled to one-half; Decedent's three heirs would each be entitled to 1/3 of his one-half. Please provide a revised proposed order (if applicable) and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/25/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Wilhelmina's date of death was apparently misstated in paragraph 27 of the petition. It would have been helpful if the accounting had had a schedule of assets on hand at the beginning.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE HARRY GLASSMAN LIVING TRUST DATED APRIL 17, 2006, as Amended and Restated
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner, Gregory Glassman'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Under Seal
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ronald E. Quidachay. Petitioner Gregory Glassman's Motion to File Under Seal is DENIED. Petitioner failed to make the requisite showing under California Rules of Court, rule 2.550(d) to justify sealing the record. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LILLIE MAE TYLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the Court to consider whether to take this petition off calendar, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to petitioner in pro per on 08/05/2024) since this petition was initially set for 08/14/2024: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) No DE-147 form is on file. 3) No DE-147S form is on file. A pleading cover sheet must be attached to this form. 4) No proof of publication is on file. 5) No DE-121 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 6) Item 2 of the petition: The petitioner's name must be specified. 7) Item 2b(3) must be checked if the decedent died intestate. 8) Item 2d: At least one box must be checked. 9) Item 3b: No foreign country was specified. 10) Item 3d(4)-(7) must be completed if the decedent owned real property subject to probate. 11) Item 3f(1) must be checked if the decedent died intestate. 12) Item 3g(2)(a) must be checked if the petitioner is the decedent's heir having priority. 13) Item 3h(1) & (3) must be checked. 14) Item 5a(3) or (4) must be checked. 15) Item 5a(5) must be checked if the petitioner is the decedent's child. 16) Item 5a(7) or (8) must be checked. 17) Item 5b: At least one box must be checked. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To 1: Determine Validity Of Trust Amendment; 2) Preclude The Use Of Trust Funds To Defend Trust Contest
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/18/2024. Issues necessitating a continuance: 1) No notice was given to the Attorney General (Probate Code Section 17203(3). 2) All notices must include language about remote appearances (San Francisco Local Rule 14.1.6(6)). 3) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court. Any party lodging a proposed order in a contested matter must file proof of service of the proposed order on all parties entitled to notice of the petition (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A(4)). Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner no later than 12/04/2024.

rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for 12/08/2025.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN MCKEON

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JUDITH ANNE MORRIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status hearing on filing the final Inventory and Appraisal will be set for 3/17/25. Status on filing either a petition for order for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. Code 12200 et seq. will be set for 11/10/25. No appearance will be necessary if all necessary documents are filed at least 2 weeks prior to the scheduled hearings.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTOPHER P. BERRY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/18/2024 (no earlier date available). Notes were e-mailed to counsel on 10/28/2024.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE NUNZIO J. CAMARDA & CATHERINE CAMARDA TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Judicial Appointment Of Trustee Of Revocable Inter-Vivos Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/6/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) Who is the successor in interest to Michael J. Camarda? [See Section 4.04 of Article Four of the trust.] Was a SNT set up for him, as directed in the amendment dated 1/8/18? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/23/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANNETTE HOLMES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing to admit will. No appearance required. Status hearing on filing of petition for final distribution or petition for approval on report of status of administration will be set for 12/08/2025.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SUMIO NAGAREDA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing Status of closing the estate will be set for 11/17/25. The administrator must file either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before that status date. Appearance will be required on 11/17/25 if no petition has been filed. (Only two printouts of unsigned Letters were provided with the courtesy copies.)

rulings.Rulings: Hearing to admit the will. No appearance required. Status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for 12/08/2025.
